regions.eastern-caribbean: Eastern Caribbean weather on March 16, 2026, features stable trade wind conditions with light to moderate easterly winds and partly cloudy skies across San Juan, St. Thomas, St. Croix, St. Maarten, St. Kitts, Antigua, and Turks & Caicos. Current winds at San Juan are 7 knots ESE with no rain expected, increasing to near 15-19 knots east to east-southeast over the next three days, keeping seas moderate but below Small Craft Advisory thresholds. Mariners should expect smooth sailing with good visibility; fishing captains will find favorable conditions for nearshore operations, but should monitor wind increases around San Juan and St. Thomas for anchorage security. Cruise passengers should plan outdoor activities with minimal rain risk, but remain aware of increasing trade winds that may cause mild sea motion on exposed decks.
regions.bahamas: Bahamas region weather for March 16, 2026: Currently in Nassau, temperatures are mild at 75°F with southeast winds at 10 knots under overcast skies and no rain expected. Today’s conditions will remain stable with light southeast to south-southeast winds 10-13 knots and minimal rain chances, suitable for cruising and fishing operations around Nassau, Freeport, Bimini, Berry Islands, and Turks & Caicos. Expect a wind shift to the north-northwest on March 17 with increased winds near 17 knots and a 68% chance of rain showers, followed by northeast winds at 18 knots and heavier showers on March 18, increasing caution for small craft and anchorage security especially in exposed areas like Bimini and Turks & Caicos. Mariners should monitor forecasts closely for increasing winds and rain, secure loose gear, and plan outdoor activities accordingly.
regions.southern-caribbean: Southern Caribbean briefing for March 16, 2026: Expect stable conditions across Aruba, Curacao, Bonaire, and Cartagena with temperatures near 80-81°F and easterly winds steady at 17-23 kt. Seas remain moderate; Small Craft Advisories may be warranted near Aruba and Curacao due to sustained winds above 20 kt, so exercise caution when navigating smaller vessels or operating near exposed anchorages. Rain chances are minimal, under 10%, with partly cloudy skies prevailing—ideal for cruise guests and fishing operations but keep a lookout for occasional gusts in open waters. Yacht captains should maintain vigilance for wind shifts in the eastern sector while docking or departing ports, particularly in Aruba where winds peak at 23 kt over the next two days.
regions.lesser-antilles: Lesser Antilles briefing for March 16, 2026: Barbados reports 79°F with ENE winds steady at 20 knots under partly cloudy skies and no rain currently. Expect continued trade winds near Small Craft Advisory thresholds through the region, with seas likely 4-6 feet; overcast skies and isolated light showers are possible especially near Martinique and Dominica. Yacht captains should secure loose gear and plan for moderate chop when transiting between ports. Fishing vessels should monitor wind shifts and avoid exposed anchorages in Antigua and St. Kitts during gusty periods. Passengers advised to carry light rain gear and stay hydrated while ashore.
regions.los-angeles: Los Angeles and nearby ports are experiencing calm, clear conditions this morning with light SSE winds at 3 knots and temperatures near 57°F. Expect a warm day with overcast skies developing by afternoon, WSW winds 8 knots, and highs near 83°F. Tomorrow will bring stronger W winds up to 10 knots and clear skies with highs approaching 92°F, followed by a cooler, overcast day Wednesday with WSW winds easing to 6 knots. Mariners should note light wind conditions favor smooth cruising or fishing today, but plan for moderate westerly winds the next two days, especially in open waters around Catalina Island. Passengers should dress in layers for variable sun exposure and occasional cloud cover.
regions.ensenada: Ensenada region conditions remain stable with light southeast winds at 1 knot and overcast skies, no precipitation expected today. Expect foggy mornings with southwest to west-southwest winds 6 knots over the next two days, reducing visibility—exercise caution when navigating near Punta Banda and Islas Todos Santos anchorages. Temperatures will rise steadily from 70°F today to 83°F by day three; no marine wind warnings are in effect. Fishing and yacht captains should monitor fog impacts on sightlines during early hours, while cruise passengers can anticipate calm seas and mild conditions for shore excursions.
regions.mazatlan: Mazatlan region conditions for March 16, 2026: Current temperature is 74°F with northwest winds at 5 knots under clear skies and no rain expected. Through the next three days, expect mild temperatures near 75°F with light variable winds from northwest to west-southwest at 6 to 9 knots, mostly partly cloudy to overcast but dry. Mariners should find calm seas and good visibility for navigation and anchoring in Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata harbors. Fishing captains can plan operations without weather interruptions; passengers should enjoy outdoor activities with minimal sun protection recommended during peak midday hours.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Puerto Vallarta region reports calm marine conditions this morning with light east winds at 4 knots and clear skies. Expect continued settled weather today with southwest winds increasing to 7 knots and clear skies; no precipitation anticipated at Puerto Vallarta, Punta Mita, Yelapa, or Chacala. Winds shift to west-northwest at 7 to 8 knots with partly cloudy skies over the next two days, maintaining stable conditions for navigation and fishing. Captains should find smooth anchorage in sheltered bays, and passengers can enjoy outdoor activities with minimal weather disruptions.
regions.western-mediterranean: Western Mediterranean weather for March 16, 2026, remains favorable for marine operations. Barcelona currently reports clear skies, 48°F, with light WNW winds at 4 knots and no precipitation expected. Over the next three days, expect overcast skies with light WNW to W winds under 6 knots on Day 1, followed by fog reducing visibility in the mornings of Days 2 and 3, with light easterly winds increasing to 15 knots on Day 3. Yacht and fishing captains should exercise caution navigating near Palma de Mallorca and Ibiza during fog episodes, ensuring radar and AIS are active. Cruise passengers can anticipate mild temperatures but should be prepared for reduced visibility conditions in port approaches, especially near Valencia and Malaga.
regions.central-mediterranean: Central Mediterranean weather on March 16, 2026, features persistent northeast winds at 7 to 14 knots with intermittent light rain and showers, notably across Naples and nearby southern ports. Expect temperatures in the mid-50s to mid-60s Fahrenheit, with increased rain chances later in the 3-day outlook, peaking near 40 percent. Mariners should maintain heightened caution when navigating coastal areas around Naples, Civitavecchia, and Livorno due to slippery decks and reduced visibility from light rain. Yacht and fishing captains are advised to monitor localized sea state changes and secure gear against periodic gusts; cruise passengers should carry rain gear and plan for limited outdoor activities during wet intervals.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Eastern Mediterranean briefing for March 16, 2026: Athens (Piraeus) is currently mild at 54°F with light WNW winds near 1 knot under partly cloudy skies and no rain expected today. Over the next three days, expect a northeast wind increase to 5-9 knots with overcast conditions and rising rain chances, reaching 40% by day three. Mariners should note generally light winds today but prepare for deteriorating conditions by midweek, especially in exposed anchorages around Santorini, Mykonos, and Rhodes where rain and NE winds will reduce visibility and sea state. Cruise and fishing captains should plan for smoother operations today but monitor updated forecasts closely for rain impacts starting tomorrow, particularly near Istanbul, Izmir, Cyprus, Haifa, and Alexandria, as wet weather may affect docking and onboard activities.
